_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C13: 122 dimension updates (CLAUDE_AI confidence), 11 Session B findings, 8 Session D pointers

set.notes: UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: description names God's inner character of just dealing as the ground of justice — divine attribute.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: human moral orientation and disposition to seek and do right.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner cry and longing directed toward God for vindication.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: inner person standing in appeal before divine judgment.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: condemnation grounded in inner state — unbelief, doubt, moral failure.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: freedom from condemnation — inner security before God.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: self-condemnation, conscience turned against the self.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral failure as ground of divine judicial consequence.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: weight of moral judgment falling on conscience.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: fearful expectation of divine verdict shaping inner life.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: culpable spiritual ignorance as inner moral condition.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: identity and standing constituted by God's prior knowing.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Cognitive/Mind: divine sovereign foreknowledge as ground of redemption.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner capacity of prophetic/divine perception.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: self-aware moral consciousness subject to God's higher judgment.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Volitional/Capacity: inward parts as seat of innermost self — the deepest layer of personhood God searches and knows.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Volitional/Capacity: experiential content — joy, pain, instruction, affliction felt in the deepest place.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: eagerness as stable inner disposition of readiness.
UNCLASSIFIED → Affective/Emotional: inner courage/cheerfulness amid crisis.
UNCLASSIFIED → Affective/Emotional: cheerfulness as affective ground of praise.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: willing spirit directed toward God in tension with flesh.
UNCLASSIFIED → Volitional/Will: eagerness as inner motivation directing toward mission.
UNCLASSIFIED → Affective/Emotional: cheerful inner composure in adversarial contexts.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner state of discouragement — loss of heart.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: eager willing service as inner orientation.
UNCLASSIFIED → Character/Disposition: covetousness as stable insatiable inner orientation toward acquisition.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: covetousness as idolatry — foundational misorientation substituting created for Creator.
UNCLASSIFIED → Character/Disposition: wilful refusal to hear — stable inner orientation of closed attention or contempt.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: disobedience as defining character-orientation of active resistance.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: disobedience as disposition preventing entry into God's rest — spiritual self-exclusion.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: disobedience as defining inner act with moral-covenantal consequence.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: inner person as object of spiritual contest — demonic snatching vs divine security.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: divine catching-away — protective/eschatological act bringing person to God.
UNCLASSIFIED → Character/Disposition: greed as stable inner condition of rapacious desire vs inner freedom.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: greedy person as moral-character type defined by insatiable acquisitive desire.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: personal confession as inner turning directed toward God.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: sustained inner orientation away from God — negative God-ward pole.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: moral wisdom about sin and inner protective posture.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: sin as burden before God addressed through divine grace.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: named offence confessed to God with prayer for covering.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: iniquity as moral debt before God — accumulated perversion God marks, bears, forgives.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: guiltlessness as inner moral condition of innocence.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: guilt as felt weight of iniquity.
UNCLASSIFIED → Affective/Emotional: experienced weight of punishment as felt from within — subjective inner burden.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inward act of perverting and committing iniquity.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: sinner as object of Christ's calling — inner condition as ground of divine mercy.
UNCLASSIFIED → Moral/Conscience: prior unrepented sin as inner moral condition Paul mourns and addresses.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: sin as offence before God addressed through repentance and cleansing.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: sin offering addressing guilt-condition; Servant's soul as fulfilment.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: sinner as moral-character type with persistent inner orientation against God.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: divine consequence/chastisement on persisters in sin.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: moral ground of inner standing — basis for guilt or innocence.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: Christ as causal ground of guilt-absence and salvation.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: conscience as inner moral witness.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: conscience as morally vulnerable.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: conscience as interior moral foundation.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: conscience as object of Christ's purification.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner event of incurring and realising guilt before God.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Cognitive/Mind: bearing guilt, inner acknowledgement, confession — moral-conscience engagement.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: guilt offering addressing guilt-condition before God; Servant's soul as supreme locus.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: self-named guilt before God.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: guiltiness as accumulated condition before God addressed through atonement.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: sin as guilt/offence before God — relational breach addressed through Christ.
UNCLASSIFIED → Volitional/Capacity: sin as indwelling power ruling and enslaving inner capacities. Note vocabulary tension.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: sin as universal human condition overcome only by God's righteousness.
UNCLASSIFIED → Moral/Conscience: inner commitment to justice in institutional form. Note: QA-EXTERNALISED.
UNCLASSIFIED → Moral/Conscience: sovereign inner orientation toward justice. Note: QA-EXTERNALISED.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Spiritual/God-ward: person's inner quality of uprightness — Moral/Conscience.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Moral/Conscience: divine attribute of justice — God's inner character.
KEYWORD_STRONG corrected from Moral/Conscience: divine act of justification transforming inner standing.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner condition of unrighteousness.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: adverbial moral alignment in conduct.
UNCLASSIFIED → Volitional/Capacity: inner response to adversary — urgency, persistence, vigilance.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner act of moral condemnation.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Spiritual/God-ward: inner moral deficit — primarily moral.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Spiritual/God-ward: justification as divine act restoring standing.
UNCLASSIFIED → Volitional/Capacity: inner act of rejecting divinely appointed authority.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral logic of appropriate retribution.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: patient endurance as inner disposition when wronged.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: divine righteous judgment confronting hard-hearted inner condition.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: inner standing of moral accountability before God.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: law as revealer of inner moral/religious orientation.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: God's inner determination to vindicate and execute justice.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral obligation for just advocacy.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: authoritative verdict shaping inner destiny.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: God's inner justice defining true judgment.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner act of perceiving and approving what is right.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: moral uprightness as inner quality.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner straightness walking before God.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: divine avenging authority and inner justice.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral distortion at community level.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: soul's affective orientation toward God's mishpat.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral-spiritual orientation of heart.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral standard for human judgment.
UNCLASSIFIED → Affective/Emotional: humility, complaint, vindication-hope as inner affective states in judicial encounter.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Cognitive/Mind: spirit-given capacity for discernment and righteous action.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner qualities required for just judging.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Moral/Conscience: inner cry to God for vindication — Spiritual/God-ward.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: inner moral condition as basis of divine judgment.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: God's righteous character expressed through just acts.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: righteousness as defining inner quality.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: righteousness as divine attribute.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: active pursuit of righteousness as inner orientation directed toward God.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner character required for just governance.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner moral commitment to just advocacy.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Volitional/Will: authoritative verdict — divine/judicial determination of guilt or innocence.
UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: persistent inner cry and appeal to God for justice.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: divine inner determination to right wrongs.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: divine vengeance from inner righteousness and human cry.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: inner moral standing before God.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner discernment required for just judicial act.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: consequence of inner condition of wickedness.
UNCLASSIFIED → Theological/Divine-Human: divine righteousness imputed as ground of human standing.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: human righteousness as inner quality.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: faith-orientation seeking righteousness through trust in God.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Relational/Social: turning from truth and grace — God-ward failure of orientation.
KEYWORD_WEAK corrected from Volitional/Will: divine immutability as ground producing inner encouragement and hope.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: irrevocable calling grounding identity.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: divine inner disposition of withheld compassion.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner act of deliberate recall and reflective turning.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: repentance as inner turning toward God.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: God's relenting as divine inner-relational response.

record.finding: H4941H mish.pat appears in both Reg 24 (condemnation) and Reg 98 (justice) — three groups each. Session B must read both registries' mish.pat groups together to form a complete inner-being profile of this term.

operations.description: Session B pointer — condemnation: H4941H mish.pat cross-registry profile

record.finding: The conscience (suneidesis/sunoida) groups in Reg 26 show a consistent pattern: moral self-awareness operating under divine authority — conscience as inner witness that remains subordinate to God's judgment. Session B should examine the relationship between conscience-as-moral-witness and divine judgment as the higher court.

operations.description: Session B pointer — conscience: inner witness under divine authority

record.finding: Reg 35 contains two analytically distinct sub-clusters: (1) willing-spirit/eager-disposition (prothumia, prothumos, prothumōs, euthumeō, euthumos, athumeō) and (2) covetous-desire proper (pleonexia). Session B should treat these as distinct inner-being phenomena requiring separate analytical attention.

operations.description: Session B pointer — covetousness: two distinct sub-clusters

record.finding: Reg 50 shows disobedience operating across three distinct inner-being dimensions in only 4 groups: Character/Disposition (defining orientation), Spiritual/God-ward (self-exclusion from divine rest), and Moral/Conscience (covenantal consequence). Session B should examine whether disobedience is primarily a dispositional, relational, or moral-act category in Scripture.

operations.description: Session B pointer — disobedience: three inner-being dimensions in compact registry

record.finding: G0726 harpazō appears in two groups with contrasting inner-being engagements: [5493-001] predatory violation of inner content (Spiritual/God-ward) and [5493-002] divine protective snatching (Theological/Divine-Human). Session B should examine whether these two uses require distinct analytical treatment or represent a unified category of inner-being vulnerability and security.

operations.description: Session B pointer — greed: harpazō dual inner-being engagements

record.finding: The guilt registry (Reg 73) shows Spiritual/God-ward as the dominant dimension — sin and guilt are primarily framed in Scripture as relational conditions before God rather than psychological states. Session B analysis should proceed from this orientation: guilt is fundamentally a God-ward condition, with Moral/Conscience and Affective/Emotional dimensions secondary.

operations.description: Session B pointer — guilt: Spiritual/God-ward as dominant dimension

record.finding: G4893 suneidesis in Reg 73 shows a four-dimensional conscience profile: (1) moral witness [53-001], (2) vulnerability [53-002], (3) interior foundation [53-003], (4) object of purification [53-004]. Session B should analyse these four functions as a coherent inner-being anatomy of conscience.

operations.description: Session B pointer — guilt: G4893 suneidesis four-dimensional conscience profile

record.finding: H4941G mish.pat (Reg 98) and H4941H mish.pat (Reg 24) are the same Hebrew term across two registries: five groups in Reg 98 and three groups in Reg 24 constitute the full inner-being profile. Session B must read both registries' mish.pat groups together for a complete analysis.

operations.description: Session B pointer — justice: H4941G mish.pat cross-registry profile with Reg 24

record.finding: The dikaios/dikaiosunē/dikaiōsis/dikaioō family (G1342, G1343, G1344, G1347) shows a consistent tension: righteousness as divine act/attribute (Theological/Divine-Human) vs righteousness as human inner quality (Moral/Conscience). This is one of the most analytically significant tensions in the programme. Session B must examine this carefully.

operations.description: Session B pointer — justice: dikaios family divine-human righteousness tension

record.finding: H6664G tsedeq and H6663 tsadeq produce four distinct groups: (1) human inner quality [942-001], (2) divine attribute [942-002], (3) pursued inner orientation [942-003], (4) moral basis for governance [942-004]. This fourfold pattern in a single Hebrew root is a major Session B analytical focus.

operations.description: Session B pointer — justice: tsedeq/tsadeq fourfold pattern

record.finding: Registry 135 shows a consistent human-divine mirroring structure: nearly every human inner-being act (turning, relenting, comforting, recalling) has a divine counterpart in the same registry (God relenting, God comforting, God withholding wrath). Session B should analyse this pairing as a key structural feature of the repentance domain in Scripture.

operations.description: Session B pointer — repentance: human-divine mirroring structure

record.description: The polarity structure of condemnation (verdict/no-verdict) is the governing framework of Reg 24 and maps onto the no-condemnation freedom of Romans 8. Session D should examine the condemnation/no-condemnation polarity as the judicial frame within which the guilt-repentance-justification arc operates across C13.

operations.description: Session D pointer — condemnation: no-condemnation polarity as judicial frame for C13

record.description: Reg 26 includes G4267 proginōskō (Identity/Selfhood) alongside conscience terms — this brings together the epistemic cluster (knowing, perception) with the identity-constituting dimension of divine foreknowing. Session D should examine whether being-known-by-God and knowing-oneself-morally form a coherent inner-being pairing that constitutes a distinct dimension of conscience.

operations.description: Session D pointer — conscience: being-known vs knowing-oneself as inner-being pairing

record.description: The positive desire vocabulary (prothumia, prothumos, prothumōs) in Reg 35 may have natural affinity with volitional vocabulary elsewhere. The contrast between eager willing (prothumia) and acquisitive desire (pleonexia) in the same registry is analytically significant for understanding how Scripture distinguishes rightly-oriented from wrongly-oriented inner desire. Session D should examine this alongside Reg 70 (greed) and the broader desire vocabulary.

operations.description: Session D pointer — covetousness/greed: rightly vs wrongly oriented inner desire

record.description: The greed vocabulary in C13 (pleonexia, pleonektēs, harpagē) shows consistent convergence on Character/Disposition. Session D should examine the relationship between greed (Reg 70), covetousness (Reg 35), and positive desire (prothumia) — the cluster's contrast between rightly- and wrongly-directed inner desire is a synthesis question.

operations.description: Session D pointer — greed: Character/Disposition convergence and desire polarity with Reg 35

record.description: The inner-being movement from guilt-incurred (Reg 73) through repentance (Reg 135) to freedom from condemnation (Reg 24) and justification (Reg 98) constitutes the governing arc of C13. Session D should examine whether this four-registry trajectory (guilt → repentance → no-condemnation → justification) maps a complete inner-being soteriology visible in Scripture.

operations.description: Session D pointer — guilt: guilt-repentance-condemnation-justification arc across C13

record.description: Reg 98 (justice) shows repeated convergence between inner moral quality (Moral/Conscience) and divine action (Theological/Divine-Human) — particularly in justification/righteousness terminology. Session D should examine the relationship between the justice cluster and the condemnation registry (Reg 24) as structurally complementary: condemnation and justification are the negative and positive poles of the same judicial framework.

operations.description: Session D pointer — justice: justification/condemnation as judicial poles with Reg 24

record.description: Two groups in Reg 98 — [3186-001] H0197H ulam (Hall of Justice) and [3188-001] H0197I ulam (Hall of Throne) — are QA-EXTERNALISED: they describe physical structures. Session D should examine the question of institutional/architectural embodiment of inner commitment to justice — to what extent does the justice vocabulary in Scripture include structural referents, and what is their inner-being significance.

operations.description: Session D pointer — justice: institutional/architectural embodiment of justice vocabulary

record.description: The divine-relenting vocabulary (na.cham, shuv divine pole) in Reg 135 is closely related to the divine-judging vocabulary in Reg 98 and the condemnation/no-condemnation polarity in Reg 24. Session D should examine whether divine relenting, divine withholding of condemnation, and divine justification constitute a unified inner-being arc — the inner person moving from guilt through repentance to freedom.

operations.description: Session D pointer — repentance: divine relenting linked to no-condemnation and justification arc
